changelog.title: What's new in Slite
changelog.text:
What's new in Slite
We value your privacy
This website or its third-party tools process personal data. You can opt out of the sale of your personal information by clicking on the “Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information” link.
Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information
Opt-out Preferences
We use third-party cookies that help us analyze how you use this website, store your preferences, and provide the content and advertisements that are relevant to you. However, you can opt out of these cookies by checking "Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information" and clicking the "Save My Preferences" button. Once you opt out, you can opt in again at any time by unchecking "Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information" and clicking the "Save My Preferences" button.

s...

Solutions
Resources
Pricing
Sign in
Book demo
Start for free
Changelog
Follow us on Linkedin
Follow us on X
Join the community
Jun 25, 2026
Slite Agent improvements
See pending approvals at a glance
— A new sidebar badge shows how many items are waiting on you, and you can jump straight to any one from its URL.
More accurate diffs
— Style-only changes, like bolding, now render correctly so you can see exactly what changed.
The agent can do more
— It can now rename docs, swap icons, and adjust layout width without touching the body. As always, these go through the standard approval flow.
Know when activity came from an agent
— Comments, activity log and doc revisions now show if an activity originated from the Slite Agent, an MCP or the API.
Public API: note ordering and HTML export options
Two additions to the public API:
Read more
Jun 9, 2026
The self-maintaining knowledge base
Meet the all-new Slite:
the first self-maintaining knowledge base
. It pairs a structured, verified knowledge base with an AI agent that detects when documentation has drifted from reality, proposes the fix, and routes every change through human approval before it becomes truth.
The Slite Agent
Slite Agent
is now available on the Pro plan. It detects when docs drift from reality across 20+ connected tools, proposes fixes, and routes every change through human review before anything is applied.
New global floating agent panel (three states: collapsed, popover, expanded) that persists across doc navigation
Triage view in the sidebar: a dedicated mode showing all pending agent runs with unread badges and per-row indicators
Agent approval right panel: click an approval card to open a 50/50 split view with the full-document diff, a minimap to jump between change hotspots, and Accept/Dismiss controls
Read more
May 27, 2026
Super is now inside Slite
AI-search (super)
If your team uses both Slite and Super, you no longer need to switch between two apps. Super is now embedded directly inside the Slite sidebar, so you can ask questions, search across all your connected tools, and get AI-powered answers without ever leaving your docs.
For Super-only customers
, the experience stays essentially the same, with only minor UX and naming adjustments.
Slite-only customers
won't notice any difference.
Alongside this, Assistants, Digests, and Buttons are now all grouped under
Agent workflows
in the sidebar, aligning with how they're presented on the website. They share a unified list layout, and the Buttons view in particular gets a cleaner look: it now shows usage counts and a tidier editing experience.
BigQuery as a data source
Read more
May 20, 2026
See every change, and know who made it
The doc history view has been completely reworked. Changes are now highlighted at the block level and down to individual words, with clear attribution to the person or agent, who made each edit. A minimap lets you jump to any section of a long doc without scrolling, and the footer has been simplified into a floating bar. The history slider now correctly starts at the document's initial state.
Super is now inside Slite
If your workspace has both Slite and Super, you can now use Super directly from the Slite sidebar, no switching apps. Ask questions, search across all your connected tools, and get answers without leaving your docs.
Slite + Super customers
: Super is now fully accessible inside the Slite app.
Slite-only customers
: Nothing changes for you.
Read more
May 13, 2026
Connect your codebase
AI-search (super)
You can now connect git repo to Super and search the context that lives alongside your code — issues, pull requests, merge requests, and markdown documentation. Ask why something was built a certain way, find the PR where a decision was made, or look up what a spec says. Your whole team gets accurate, up-to-date answers without waiting on a developer.
No data is stored in Super; your repo permissions stay exactly where they are.
Connect Git →
Follow up on any digest
Every scheduled digest is now saved to your Super history. Click through from your inbox to view the full report in Super and ask follow-up questions — dig into the numbers, filter by team, or explore what's behind a trend. Every report is a starting point, not a dead end.
Read more
May 6, 2026
New boxed layout
You may have noticed something feels different in Slite. The main content area now sits in a white box surrounded by lighter sidebars, giving Slite a cleaner, more focused look. Search and Ask are now regular sidebar row items, a small change that sets the stage for bigger things coming in June. The layout adapts cleanly to mobile, and the sidebar shell has been unified across Slite and Super so both products share the same design primitives going forward.
Comments redesigned
Comments now appear in document order.
A new
Unread
tab makes it easy to catch up.
Notification emails now show the highlighted text that was commented on.
If you @-mention someone who doesn't have access to the doc, you'll get a warning before it goes out.
Read more
Apr 29, 2026
Digests just got a major upgrade
AI-search (super)
Digests used to send a single global report to everyone, based on the intersection of permissions. Now they're personalized per recipient, scoped to what each person actually has access to.
You can now chain steps together to gather, summarize, and format data in one run.
The new engine handles far larger volumes than before. For example, it can now process up to 3,000 Intercom conversations per run, up from ~150–200.
Every scheduled run is saved to your Super history, so recipients can revisit past reports and ask follow-up questions to dig deeper into the data.
The digest editor has moved into the main Super workflow editor, giving you one place to build both assistants and digests.
Transparent data source permissions
Read more
Apr 22, 2026
MCP: Read and resolve comment threads
Slite's MCP integration now exposes comment threads on documents. Any MCP client — Claude, Cursor, your own agent — can now:
List all threads
on a note, with full comment history, participants, and resolved state
Fetch a specific thread
by ID, or by note + thread ID together
Resolve or unresolve
a thread programmatically
This opens up powerful agentic workflows: an AI can now read the discussion context on a design doc, triage open threads, or mark resolved ones as done — without any human having to open the document first.
Find in Collections
Read more
Apr 15, 2026
Salesforce as a source
AI-search (super)
You can now connect
Salesforce
to Super and ask questions across your CRM in plain language — deal status, account history, pipeline, contacts, and much more. Super queries Salesforce in real-time; no data is stored in Super, so your permissions and audit logs stay exactly where they are.
Connect Salesforce →
Assistant workflow editor improvements
Building and editing assistants just got a lot simpler. Steps are now expanded by default so nothing is hidden, step names are inferred automatically from your instructions so you don't have to name them manually, and the welcome message, kickstart buttons, and context box are now clearly separated — making it much easier to see and configure what your assistant actually does. All existing assistants have been migrated automatically.
Slack auto-sync
Super now
automatically syncs new public Slack channels
as they're created. For private channels, any team member can invite
@Super
directly, no admin needed.
Read more
Apr 8, 2026
Multi-Column Layouts
You can now place content side by side in your docs. Type
/column
to pick up to 4 columns, or drag existing blocks next to each other — no command needed. Columns are great for comparing options, building compact summaries, or giving long documents a more structured and scannable layout. Layouts adapt to mobile viewports too, so your docs look great wherever your team reads them.
Read more about how to use columns in Slite
Knowledge Management Panel
The Knowledge Management Panel has been completely rewritten from the ground up. You can now:
Export any view as a CSV
— perfect for large audits where you need to divide work across a team.
Read more
Mar 16, 2026
Slite Ask, now powered by the Super engine
Ask has been upgraded to run on the same AI engine that powers
Super
. This brings a significant improvement in answer quality, reliability, and speed.
Answers are more accurate and more consistent
Ask is now available on public docs
No custom API key required — Ask uses our optimized setup by default
The experience has been refreshed with a new UI closer to Super's
In-editor AI features: rebuilt from the ground up
Read more
Feb 25, 2026
Super MCP
AI-search (super)
You can now connect Super to
Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, Gamma, Lovable,
or any MCP-compatible tool to get the most exhaustive and accurate company search, wherever you work.
You can add Super MCP with the following URL:
https://api.super.work/mcp
and
learn more on our help center
.
Assistant analytics & discovery
You now have visibility into which assistants your team actually uses — and the best ones surface automatically.
Sorted by popularity
— most used team assistants surface first, along your favorite ones.
Usage metrics at glance
— instantly see the usage from last 90 days in the assistants list.
Read more
Feb 15, 2026
Slite MCP Server
Connect AI assistants like Claude and ChatGPT directly to your Slite workspace. Search docs, create notes, and update content through natural language—all while respecting your existing permissions.
Our MCP server follows the authenticated remote
MCP spec
, so it's centrally hosted and uses OAuth for secure authentication. No API keys or config files to manage.
Connect via
https://api.slite.com/mcp
in Claude, ChatGPT, and other MCP-compatible clients.
Available tools include:
Search and retrieve docs with advanced filtering
Create and update notes with full formatting support
Read more
Jan 13, 2026
Smarter conversations
AI-search (super)
Since V2, we've been focused on making Super conversations feel smoother and data sources more reliable, while preparing our next major launches.
Slack improvements
Super steps in Slack bot
— You can now have visibility into what Super is doing while the answer is being generated.
Sources in slack answers are also now rendered as
clickable links
and we fixed crashes when answers exceeded the maximum size of Slack messages.
Reliable conversations
Chat titles in the sidebar
— Your history now shows readable titles like
"Mobile redesign status"
or
"Issues assigned to me"
.
Read more
Jan 6, 2026
New: Protected docs, easier-to-read tables, and smarter defaults
As workspaces grow, keeping knowledge consistent, protected, and easy to manage becomes essential. This update focuses on stronger document protection, clearer defaults, and smoother collaboration across your workspace.
Protect docs and their sub-docs
You can now apply protected editing to a doc and automatically extend it to all of its sub-docs.
Choose to protect a single doc or a doc and all its children
Prevent accidental edits across documentation hierarchies
Useful for shared, long-lived, or critical docs
Read more
Nov 13, 2025
Super V2 is here
AI-search (super)
We've completely rebuilt Super from the ground up. V2 transforms Super from a search tool to a work orchestration platform.
It also radically increases speed, accuracy, and brings the ability to build complex workflows in plain language.
Learn more about Super v2 →
Agentic search
Super v2 introduces
multi-step orchestration
that breaks down complex queries into logical steps while accelerating simpler ones. What used to take multiple searches across different tools can now be orchestrated in a single workflow.
Read more
Oct 26, 2025
Maintain your knowledge with ease
In the age of AI, trusted knowledge is the key to unlocking your team and AI workflows. That's what this update is all about.
API updates: Programmatic knowledge management
New endpoints give you and your agents full control over document lifecycle and trustworthiness:
PUT /notes/{noteId}/verify
- Mark documents as verified
PUT /notes/{noteId}/flag-as-outdated
- Flag outdated content
PUT /notes/{noteId}/owner
- Assign document ownership
Read more
Oct 7, 2025
New: Collapsible Sections
You can now add collapsible sections to organize long docs and make them easier to scan.
Collapse or expand sections anywhere in your doc
Headers stay visible even when content is hidden
Works with all block types — text, lists, images, tables, sketches
Collapsed by default on refresh to keep things neat
Improved markdown imports
Read more
Sep 14, 2025
A better way to fix knowledge gaps
AI-search (super)
We've redesigned the Ask Insights experience to help you identify and fix knowledge gaps across all your connected tools:
New side-by-side comparison view
showing original and improved answers
Improved access controls
for managing who can view Ask Insights
Direct source correction for Slite
-if you use Slite knowledge base, you can open docs in

analysis.name: Slite
analysis.value_proposition: An AI-powered knowledge base that stays synced with your tools and is verified by your team, serving as the trusted context layer for humans and AI agents.
analysis.ideal_customer: AI-native teams at growing companies (3,000+ leading companies) needing a trusted single source of truth for docs across IT & Operations, Product & Engineering, Customer Support, and HR

landing.title: Mem – Your AI Thought Partner
landing.text:
Mem – Your AI Thought Partner
Product
keyboard_arrow_down
Solutions
keyboard_arrow_down
Pricing
Log in
Download
One place for everything on your mind
Dump your ideas, meetings, and research into Mem—and get it all organized for you.
Download Mem
Watch the video
Remembering is so yesterday
We pour so much into not forgetting. You could keep using chaotic systems—or—you could let Mem learn how you think and do the remembering for you.
Get things out of your head and into Mem
Dump anything into Mem and rest assured you’ll find it easily later.
With your voice
Turn brain dumps into beautifully organized notes.
In meetings
Let Mem record, transcribe, and take meeting notes for you.
On the web
Save any website in a single click with the Mem Chrome Extension.
On the go
Shoot Mem a quick message with the info you want to remember.
Mem helps you use everything you save
Mem remembers and organizes for you, bringing up past notes and info so nothing goes to waste.
No-effort organizing
Quickly throw notes into collections, or just ask Mem to organize them for you.
See related context without asking
Didn’t organize? No problem. Heads Up automatically brings up related timelines, notes, collections
Mem understands and answers questions about your notes
Mem knows what’s related, quickly connecting relevant details and topics.
Find needle-in-a-haystack info
Find that thing on the tip of your tongue, even without keywords. If you can describe it, you can find it.
Chat with your mind’s better half
Trust Mem to help you use your notes to the fullest while answering questions or drafting content.
Do more with Mem
Get handy tips and tricks on the
The Memo

landing.text:
Slab - Knowledge Base & Wiki Software
Product
Resources
Library
Pricing
Log In
➔
Menu
Build a culture of knowledge-sharing today.
Easily create, organize, and discover knowledge for your entire organization, from non-technical to tech-savvy.
Sign up with Google
or
Get Started
Free up to 10 users
Read our customer stories
Create
Create content that looks good by default.
Slab features the modern editing capabilities you’ve come to expect, all within an intuitive interface. Your content on Slab looks good by default, so you can focus on the writing, not fixing the formatting.
Organize
Make it easy for teammates to browse and discover.
Folders and tags are not enough. Slab Topics both organize and provide relevant context to make it easy for your team to learn about company policies and procedures, and each others' ideas and insights.
Search
Not yet another place to search.
With Unified Search, we'll pull answers from your Slab content
and
all of your integrated tools, bringing you the most relevant results — all in one place.
Integrate
Integrate with your favorite tools.
Slab is a knowledge base, pure and simple. It's a best-in-class product that isn't trying to replace the rest of your stack. That's why it integrates with everything else, including your spreadsheets, slide decks, flowcharts, task managers, and much more.
Switching?
We’ll take care of that for you

Frequently Asked Questions
Not seeing your question here?
Email us
Chat with us
Can I use Slab for free?
Yes! Slab is free to for up to 10 users + guests forever. We think you'll love it, whichever plan you are on!
Are there discounts for non-profits or educational use?
Yes! Slab's Startup plan is free for non-profits and educational use. Register for a
free account
and then
request the discount
.
How are users counted for billing?
You will be billed for the number of users who can log into Slab. Admins may deactivate users at any time.
How are guests different from regular users?
Guests only have access to the topics, and posts within topics, that they are a member of. Regular users by default have access to all open topics and published posts.
Learn more about guests
.
Are there readonly users?
No, edit access is based on how content is organized and shared. Slab does not have a user account that inherently cannot edit anything.
What happens if I am not satisfied with Slab?
We offer a 30-day money-back guarantee. No questions asked.

analysis.name: Slab
analysis.tagline: Build a culture of knowledge-sharing today.
analysis.value_proposition: A dedicated knowledge base and wiki that integrates with your existing tools to let teams create, organize, and discover company knowledge without replacing the rest of your stack.
analysis.ideal_customer: Teams of 10–500 who want a focused, clean knowledge base that sits alongside (rather than replacing) tools like Slack, GitHub, and Google Workspace

features.name: posts
features.description: Rich-text documents for capturing and sharing knowledge, with modern editing defaults

features.name: topics
features.description: Hierarchical organization unit that provides both structure and contextual framing for related posts
features.evidence_quote: Slab Topics both organize and provide relevant context to make it easy for your team to learn

features.name: private topics
features.description: Topics visible only to invited members, hiding sensitive content from the broader team

features.name: unified search
features.description: Single search that returns results from both Slab content and all connected third-party tools
features.evidence_quote: pull answers from your Slab content and all of your integrated tools, bringing you the most relevant results

features.name: realtime collaboration
features.description: Simultaneous multi-user editing of posts in real time

features.name: templates
features.description: Pre-built post templates for common knowledge types (code reviews, onboarding, RFCs, etc.)

features.name: verification
features.description: Mark posts as verified/accurate to signal content trustworthiness and staleness

features.name: version history
features.description: Track and restore previous versions of posts; retention window varies by plan

features.name: usage analytics
features.description: Dashboard showing content engagement and readership metrics; window varies by plan
features.evidence_quote: 30 days Usage Analytics … 90 days … 365 days … Unlimited

features.name: custom domains
features.description: Host your Slab knowledge base under a custom company domain

features.name: ai autofix
features.description: AI-powered writing correction and polish for post content

features.name: ai predict
features.description: AI-driven predictive writing assistance to speed up content creation

features.name: ai ask
features.description: Conversational AI Q&A against your knowledge base; advanced version on Enterprise

features.name: sso
features.description: Single sign-on login for team members across all plans

features.name: saml-based sso
features.description: SAML 2.0 enterprise identity provider integration for SSO

features.name: scim provisioning
features.description: Automated user provisioning and deprovisioning via SCIM protocol

features.name: audit logs
features.description: Full activity log for compliance and security review; Enterprise-only

features.name: graphql api
features.description: GraphQL API for programmatic access to Slab content and structure

features.name: webhooks
features.description: Event-driven webhooks to push Slab activity to external systems

features.name: guest access
features.description: Limited-access accounts for external collaborators scoped to specific topics
features.evidence_quote: Guests only have access to the topics, and posts within topics, that they are a member of

features.name: standard integrations
features.description: Embed and sync with standard third-party tools; capped at 10 on free plan

features.name: premium integrations
features.description: Deep integrations with Microsoft, Okta, OneLogin, Zendesk; gated on Business/Enterprise

features.name: desktop app
features.description: Native desktop application for Mac/Windows

features.name: chrome extension
features.description: Browser extension for quick access to and capture into Slab from Chrome

features.name: import & export
features.description: Import content, structure, and users from other tools; export Slab content out
features.evidence_quote: Import content, structure and users into Slab, or take them out with export

features.name: users & groups
features.description: Manage team members and group them for permissions and content targeting
features.evidence_quote: Users & Groups — Get to know your teammates

features.name: admin controls
features.description: Admin panel for configuring Slab workspace settings, billing, and user management
features.evidence_quote: Admin — Set Slab up for your team — 16 articles

features.name: uptime sla
features.description: Contractual uptime SLA for Business and Enterprise customers

features.name: attachment storage
features.description: File attachment support with per-file size limits that scale by plan (10MB–100MB+)

features.name: content embeds
features.description: Embed content from 50+ external tools (Figma, Loom, Miro, etc.) directly inside posts
features.evidence_quote: Integrations & Embeds — Connect Slab with all the other tools you use and love — 57 articles

features.name: user deactivation
features.description: Admins can deactivate users at any time to stop billing without deleting content
features.evidence_quote: Admins may deactivate users at any time.

differentiators: Unified Search that surfaces results from Slab and all integrated tools in one place
Topics provide organizational context beyond folders and tags
Explicitly positioned as best-in-class knowledge base that doesn't try to replace the rest of your stack
Free plan available forever for up to 10 users with no credit card required
Startup plan offered free for non-profits and educational organizations

free_tier.limits: 10 users, 3 guests/user, 10MB attachments, 90-day version history, 10 standard integrations

tiers.name: Free
tiers.headline_inclusions: Up to 10 users, unlimited posts/topics, realtime collab, templates, unified search, basic SSO

tiers.name: Startup
tiers.headline_inclusions: Unlimited users, private topics, AI Autofix, 365-day version history, unlimited standard integrations

tiers.name: Business
tiers.headline_inclusions: SAML SSO, SCIM, AI Predict + Ask, GraphQL API, webhooks, 3 premium integrations, uptime SLA

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: 100+ users, audit logs, unlimited premium integrations, advanced AI Ask, dedicated support, custom attachments

pricing.notes: Annual billing shown; monthly rates available but not captured in source. Startup plan is free for non-profits and educational orgs. Monthly prices not shown in source data — only annual rates captured. 30-day money-back guarantee on paid plans. Enterprise requires minimum 100 users.

GitBook Agent monitors your docs, proactively suggesting improvements to ensure your users find accurate, up-to-date information every time.

Beta
AI-Ready Docs
The knowledge layer for AI agents
Built-in MCP gives agents structured access to your docs. Agent analytics show you which tools are querying, how often, and exactly what they asked.
“GitBook helps every department — it’s just easy cross-collaboration. One tool does it all, and we don’t have to hold somebody’s hand through the docs process. It’s made life very easy.”
Gareth Brinn

Embedded assistant
A connected, personalized AI Assistant

Docs tools for teams who care about quality
Streamline your docs processes with a better collaboration workflow and proactive, relevant AI suggestions
Git Sync
A workflow your entire team will love
Edit in the visual editor, your IDE, or both — with everything perfectly in sync with GitHub or GitLab. Why compromise on a workflow that doesn’t work for everyone?
Git Sync
VISUAL EDITOR
A best-in-class editing experience
Meet the visual editor that is so good even technical users prefer it. With reviews, merge rules and built-in AI linting tools to correct and polish your content.
GitBook Editor
GitBook AGENT
Team up with a proactive partner
GitBook Agent learns from support tickets, changelogs and repos automatically — then proactively suggests and generates improvements ready for your team to review.
GitBook Agent

Built to perform
GitBook sites are stable and reliable, with consistently high uptime and performance. So your users can always find the knowledge they need with no frustration.

Selma Bennamane

Batch

Connected Knowledge
A connected layer for product knowledge
Unify every knowledge source — from product updates and community feedback to on-team expertise — into one intelligent layer that lives in your docs, in your product, and far beyond.
Enterprise-grade docs intelligence
GitBook scales with your team — offering the security, control and flexibility that modern organizations need
Learn more
Migration and support
White-glove migration, 1:1 support & training, and custom integrations ensure you get up, running and ready to scale fast.
Access control
Tiered role and permissions settings let you precisely choose who can view and edit your content.
Security and compliance
We are SOC 2, ISO 27001 and GDPR compliant, with SAML-based SSO to meet your security and data requirements.
Auth-protected content
With authenticated access, only your chosen customers, team members or authorized users can view your docs.

“GitBook is easy to use, and beautiful to look at. Its no-code styling and wide range of tools for displaying and organizing information make it easy for us to maintain our documentation as if it’s a product in its own right.”
Sofie Penn-Slater
,
Team Lead, Customer Education
Channable
Read the story
View the docs
CUSTOM
EXPERIENCES
Build custom components
Use GitBook’s React-inspired developer platform to build custom components for all kinds of use-cases — from embeddable widgets to components from your design system.
Build your own
Built-in extensions
BRANDING
Create docs that match your brand
Make your published docs feel like a seamless part of your product experience using extensive customization and localization options.
Customize your docs site
“The GitBook design looks good out of the box, with many options to customize it further. A consistent experience across the product is key, and we enjoyed being able to make our docs on-brand.”
Ron Cohen
,
CTO & co-founder
Reflag
Read the story
View the docs
Connect to your existing stack
EMBED
Work with your favorite tools
Embed your favorite tools directly into your docs pages, allowing you to add interactive code sandboxes, diagrams, forms and more.
EXTEND
Track analytics
Add analytics and support integrations to measure success and help your users get the answers they need faster than ever.
Connect to your favorite sources
Extend GitBook by connecting different sources to power up GitBook Assistant’s answers or give more context to GitBook Agent.
Connect to GitBook Assistant
Connect third-party sources like GitHub, YouTube and your website to the AI Assistant. Now it can add extra context to answers, suggesting related videos or links for further reading.
Channels for GitBook Agent
Add GitBook Agent to tools like GitHub, Linear and Slack, then tag @gitbook in any PR, issue or thread. The Agent will scan the context and propose docs changes in GitBook, ready for you to review.
Git Sync
Set up a two-way sync with a GitHub or GitLab repo to combine the best of docs-as-code workflows with GitBook’s unbeatable visual editor. Now your whole team can contribute to a single source of truth.
MCP
GitBook automatically creates an MCP server for every docs site as soon as you publish. When users connect it to their AI tools, they can get accurate knowledge pulled from right from your docs without switching tools.
“We love how easy it is to work with the GitBook team. If there’s a feature that we want, they either build it, or show us how existing features can help. GitBook has always been a great partner to us.”
Gareth Brinn
,
Documentation Manager
Gravitee
Read the story
View the docs
Built-in extensions
GitBook’s built-in extensions let you embed interactive content directly on the page, or expand your site’s functionality through platforms like GitHub, LaunchDarkly, Intercom and more.

hits.title: Logseq: Privacy-First, Joyful Platform for Knowledge Management
Logseq – privacy-first, open-source knowledge sharing and management platform
After Obsidian and Logseq, I give Dendron a try
Let's build a decentralized social network together with Logseq
SilverBullet – note-taking Git-friendly alternative to Logseq and Obsidian
Show HN: Logseq – A roam like open-source plaintext note-taking app
Logseq Raises $4.1M to Accelerate Growth of the New World Knowledge Graph
Logseq – adding settings for self-hosted sync

analysis.name: Logseq
analysis.value_proposition: A privacy-first, open-source outliner that stores all notes as local plain-text files and links them into a personal knowledge graph.
analysis.ideal_customer: Knowledge workers, students, academics, writers, and developers who want full data ownership and a Roam-style outliner without cloud lock-in.

features.name: local-first storage
features.description: All notes stored as plain-text files on the user's own device; no mandatory cloud.
features.evidence_quote: You own your data locally forever

features.name: open-source codebase
features.description: Entire application is open-source and free for personal use.
features.evidence_quote: Open source / Free forever for personal use

features.name: markdown support
features.description: Notes are stored as standard Markdown files, readable by other tools.
features.evidence_quote: Document formats: Markdown and Org Mode

features.name: org-mode support
features.description: Alternative document format using Emacs Org Mode syntax.
features.evidence_quote: Document formats: Markdown and Org Mode

features.name: daily journals
features.description: Built-in daily journal page for capturing time-stamped notes and tasks.
features.evidence_quote: Getting started with the Journals page

features.name: backlinks
features.description: Automatic reverse links shown on any page that is referenced elsewhere.

features.name: linked references
features.description: Panel showing all pages/blocks that link to the current page.
features.evidence_quote: Capture, structure, and review all of your class notes with ease using the Linked References

features.name: unlinked references
features.description: Surfaces mentions of a page that have not yet been formally linked.

features.name: block references
features.description: Embed or reference any individual block by ID across the graph.

features.name: block embeds
features.description: Inline live-rendering of another block's content inside the current page.
features.evidence_quote: The difference between block embeds and block references

features.name: page embeds
features.description: Inline live-rendering of an entire page's content inside another page.
features.evidence_quote: The difference between block embeds and page embeds

features.name: page aliases
features.description: Define alternate names for a page so links to either resolve to the same node.

features.name: properties
features.description: Key-value metadata attached to pages or blocks for structured data.

features.name: task management
features.description: Built-in TODO/DONE task states with priorities and scheduling on any block.

features.name: queries
features.description: Datalog-powered queries to surface and aggregate blocks matching any criteria.
features.evidence_quote: Use queries to generate tables of relevant information.

features.name: query builder
features.description: Visual UI for constructing queries without writing raw Datalog.

features.name: advanced queries
features.description: Full Datalog query language for complex cross-graph data retrieval.

features.name: templates
features.description: Reusable block/page templates that can be inserted on demand.

features.name: pdf highlights
features.description: Annotate PDFs directly inside Logseq with highlights linked back into the graph.

features.name: whiteboards
features.description: Infinite canvas for placing and connecting notes, blocks, and new content spatially.
features.evidence_quote: Place any of your thoughts from the knowledge base or new ones next to each other on an infinite canvas

features.name: flashcards
features.description: Spaced-repetition flashcard system built on graph content for memorization.
features.evidence_quote: Remember facts and insights using the Flashcards feature.

features.name: knowledge graph view
features.description: Visual node-link graph showing relationships between all pages in the graph.

features.name: plugins
features.description: Community plugin ecosystem (150+) for extending core functionality.
features.evidence_quote: 150+ Plugins / Extend functionality to your needs

features.name: custom themes
features.description: 30+ community-built themes to customize the UI look and feel.

features.name: search
features.description: Full-text search across all pages and blocks via CMD-K shortcut.
features.evidence_quote: Use CMD-K to search with ease.

features.name: encrypted sync
features.description: End-to-end encrypted cloud sync (Logseq Sync) keeping notes up-to-date across devices.
features.evidence_quote: With encrypted file syncing, you'll always have your notes backed up and securely available in real-time

features.name: self-hosted sync
features.description: Option to configure a self-hosted sync server instead of Logseq's cloud.
features.evidence_quote: Logseq – adding settings for self-hosted sync

features.name: mobile apps
features.description: Native iOS and Android apps for capturing and reading notes on mobile.
features.evidence_quote: Mobile apps / Available for iOS & Android

features.name: slide presentation
features.description: Convert a page's outline into a slide deck presentation.

features.name: calculator
features.description: Inline calculator blocks for arithmetic within notes.

features.name: tables
features.description: Structured table creation within pages.

features.name: draw
features.description: Embedded freehand drawing/sketching tool within notes.

features.name: media embeds
features.description: Embed audio, photos, and video files directly in note blocks.

features.name: zotero integration
features.description: Native integration with Zotero reference manager to pull citations and PDFs.

features.name: publishing
features.description: Publish a Logseq graph or subset of pages as a public website.

features.name: keyboard shortcuts
features.description: Comprehensive keyboard shortcut system for navigation and editing.

features.name: right-hand sidebar
features.description: Persistent sidebar panel for opening linked pages and blocks alongside the main view.
features.evidence_quote: How to work with the right-hand sidebar

features.name: commands
features.description: Slash-command and advanced command palette for inserting content and triggering actions.

features.name: hiccup
features.description: Clojure-style Hiccup syntax for writing raw HTML-like structures inside notes.

features.name: localization
features.description: UI translated into many languages by the community.

features.name: indentation / outliner
features.description: Hierarchical block outliner with infinite nesting via indentation.
features.evidence_quote: What is indentation and why does it matter?

features.name: linked references filtering
features.description: Filter the linked references panel by page, tag, or property to narrow context.

features.name: plugin api
features.description: Public plugin development API enabling community-built extensions.
features.evidence_quote: Create a plugin! Start with Plugins 101

features.name: existing markdown graph import
features.description: Open an existing folder of Markdown files as a Logseq graph without migration.
features.evidence_quote: How to create a Logseq graph using existing Markdown files

differentiators: Privacy-first: all data stored locally as plain-text files, no mandatory cloud
Open-source codebase — free forever for personal use
Markdown/Org Mode files openable in any other tool
End-to-end encrypted sync (Logseq Sync) with self-hosted option
Outliner-first structure with bi-directional linking at block granularity
Raised $4.1M to build a 'new world knowledge graph'

recent_ships.what: Whiteboards (BETA) — infinite canvas for spatial note connections
recent_ships.source: landing page label: 'Whiteboards NEW BETA'

recent_ships.what: Logseq Sync (BETA) — encrypted cross-device file syncing
recent_ships.source: landing page label: 'Logseq Sync BETA'

recent_ships.what: Self-hosted sync settings added
recent_ships.source: HN: 'Logseq – adding settings for self-hosted sync'

recent_ships.what: $4.1M seed raise to accelerate the knowledge graph platform
recent_ships.source: HN: 'Logseq Raises $4.1M to Accelerate Growth of the New World Knowledge Graph'

praised: Daily notes and meeting notes workflow: 'I mostly use it for work: keeping daily notes & meeting notes. It's the best solution I've found to managing my tasks' (oskr via Discord)
Privacy-first local storage praised heavily on HN (276↑ on privacy-first announcement post)
Open-source ethos and community-driven development
Roam-like outliner with bi-directional links for free
complaints: Performance / complexity noted as a friction point compared to alternatives like Obsidian (HN thread: 'After Obsidian and Logseq, I give Dendron a try')
User notes using only 'a fraction of the features' — feature surface seen as overwhelming for some
Sync reliability questions (self-hosted sync settings thread on HN suggests cloud sync needed work)

free_tier.limits: unlimited local graphs; personal use; no seat or storage cap

tiers.name: Personal (Free)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Full desktop + mobile app, all core features, local storage, open-source forever

tiers.name: Logseq Sync
tiers.headline_inclusions: Encrypted cross-device sync (BETA); price not publicly disclosed in scraped sources

pricing.notes: Pricing page was not scraped; Logseq Sync is in BETA and pricing details are unknown from available sources. Core app is free/open-source with no seat limits. A self-hosted sync option exists for users who want to avoid Logseq's cloud entirely.

The enterprise AI knowledge solution custom fit to you
Get a package built around your scale, knowledge complexity, and AI maturity — with the expertise to make it work from day one.
Book a call with our team
Watch an overview
How support teams use Guru to drive better outcomes

Here's what a package looks like:
The Platform
AI Knowledge Layer
A self-improving knowledge platform that structures, verifies, and delivers trusted answers across your entire stack.
Automated knowledge quality & maintenance
Knowledge Agents for cited, permission-aware answers
AI search grounded in governed knowledge
100+ integrations and MCP delivery
Verification workflows and usage signals
Deep research across your knowledge layer
The Expertise
AI & KM Strategy Team
A team of solution engineers because AI moves fast, and you'll move faster with experts who've seen what works.
Knowledge architecture design
AI agent configuration and tuning
Integration setup and rollout support
Change management playbook
Ongoing optimization reviews
AI strategy advisory aligned to your roadmap
You stay autonomous in day-to-day ops
The Infrastructure
Enterprise Governance
Centralized policy enforcement, permissions inheritance, and audit trails across every knowledge consumer — human and AI.
SOC 2 Type II, HIPAA, GxP compliance
SSO, SCIM, and role-based access
Data Loss Prevention (DLP) Masking
Data encryption at rest and in transit
Permission-aware answers with citations and lineage
Centralized audit logs across all consumers
Your data never trains our AI models
Frequently Asked Q's
How does Guru pricing work?
Guru is a platform and expertise solution — not just a per-seat tool. Your investment is tailored to your organization's scale, knowledge complexity, and AI maturity. It includes full platform access, access to a team of solution engineers, knowledge architecture design, and ongoing optimization. Our team will scope the right engagement during your first conversation.
What does the expertise layer include?
Think of it as embedding a KM and AI strategy team into your organization. We design your knowledge architecture, configure and tune your AI agents, build your rollout playbook, and provide ongoing advisory on how to get the most out of your AI investments. Your team retains full control of the Guru environment — our expertise accelerates your time-to-value and ensures you're set up correctly from day one. It's a launchpad, not an ongoing dependency.
How is Guru different from Glean, Copilot, or building our own?
Other platforms connect to your data and retrieve what's there — as-is. Guru structures, governs, and continuously improves your knowledge so it gets more accurate over time. Corrections propagate everywhere. Governance is centralized, not per-tool. And Guru powers your existing AI tools via MCP rather than competing with them. Plus, you get the strategy and expertise to make it work — not just an instruction manual.
What integrations does Guru support?
Guru connects to 100+ enterprise tools out of the box — Slack, Microsoft Teams, Salesforce, Zendesk, Confluence, SharePoint, and more. Through MCP, your AI tools and agents pull from the same governed knowledge layer without rebuilding permissions or governance per tool.
Take a look at our
integrations
. If something you need isn't there yet, let us know—we're always adding new flavors to our integrations lineup.
What security and compliance standards does Guru meet?
Guru is audited annually for SOC 2, Type II, Microsoft 365 Certification and the Google Cloud App Security Assessment framework (CASA). In addition, Guru has crosswalked its family of standards to industry requirements such as GDPR, HIPAA, HECVAT and CAIQ and will support those customers accordingly. Guru implements a host of protections and best practices for data security. All data is encrypted at rest and in transit. Permissions are inherited from your existing systems and enforced in real time. Every answer includes citations and audit trails, and your data never trains an AI LLM.
Can I see Guru before committing?
Absolutely. Our first conversation is a working session — not a sales pitch. We'll walk through how Guru fits your stack, your governance requirements, and your AI roadmap with real use cases relevant to your organization.
Do you have non-profit pricing?
Yes! Guru for Good is a Give First initiative that offers Guru at a reduced cost to eligible organizations.  If your team is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit reach out for more info.
Thank you for your dedicated service to others. 💚

analysis.name: Guru
analysis.tagline: The Governed Knowledge Layer for Enterprise AI
analysis.value_proposition: Guru structures, governs, and continuously improves company knowledge so every AI tool and person gets trusted, cited, permission-aware answers.
analysis.ideal_customer: Enterprises deploying AI at scale that need a governed, verified source of truth across scattered knowledge systems (support, HR, IT, sales, ops).

features.name: knowledge agents
features.description: Configurable AI agents that deliver cited, permission-aware answers grounded in governed knowledge.
features.evidence_quote: Knowledge Agents for cited, permission-aware answers

features.name: knowledge agent skills
features.description: Reusable skill templates (e.g., IT troubleshooting, acronym handling) that extend agent behavior.
features.evidence_quote: Knowledge Agent Skills / Skill Template: Known Issues Card Creator

features.name: knowledge agent automations
features.description: Automated workflows powered by Knowledge Agents.

features.name: deep research

features.evidence_quote: AI search grounded in governed knowledge

features.description: Knowledge Agents automatically verify and unverify info based on usage and policy.

features.name: usage spike detection

differentiators: Governed knowledge layer that structures and continuously improves knowledge rather than just retrieving it as-is
Corrections propagate everywhere across all connected AI tools and consumers
Powers existing AI tools via MCP rather than competing with them
Embedded AI & KM strategy team included, not just software
Centralized governance and permissions across every knowledge consumer (human or AI)
Auto-verification of content driven by usage signals and policy
Customer data never trains Guru's AI models

Case studies cite 90% adoption and 100% trust at Lemonade, and support-volume reductions at Steno and Shopify

complaints: No substantive product complaints surfaced in provided sources; G2 review body was not returned and HN threads are unrelated to the product

tiers.name: Enterprise AI Knowledge Solution (custom package)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Platform + embedded AI/KM strategy team + enterprise governance; scoped to org scale, knowledge complexity, and AI maturity

pricing.notes: Guru has moved off transparent per-seat pricing to a single contact-sales package. Investment is tailored to scale, knowledge complexity, and AI maturity, and bundles software with a solution-engineering/strategy team; no free tier or self-serve plan is listed.

Turn a blank page into a polished draft with Gemini in Docs
Using a few simple prompts, create professional, stylized, and structured documents. Gemini in Docs synthesizes information from your Drive, Gmail, and Chat to build full drafts in moments. Then, use Match Format and Match Writing Style to match your company’s branding and your tone.
Effortlessly create documents
Never start a document from scratch - get started with templates and building blocks to get a professional look from the start.
Get a jumpstart on your document with smart canvas
Instantly build formatted emails, calendar invites, review trackers, meeting notes, and more in Docs when you type ‘@.’
Easily populate your document with rich details
Use smart chips to add names, files, calendar events, dropdown menus, and more when you type ‘@.’
Remove page breaks and write without limits
Choose pageless mode to create content - continuously write and scroll without interruption.
Stay organized with document tabs
Create a tab for each part of a project, plan, or report and keep everything in one place - never lose track of your documents again.
Collaborate on and refine your document, from anywhere
Stay in sync with edits and comments from internal and external teams on your phone, tablet, or web browser.
Meet directly in your documents
Premium feature
This feature is available on some Google Workspace
Business and Enterprise
plans.
Collaborate in context by meeting in Google Docs,
Sheets
, or
Slides
.
Control access to your documents
Easy-to-manage permissions gives you granular control over who can edit, comment, download, share, or view your documents.
Collaborate with your partners and make decisions, all in one place
Use comments and emojis to give feedback to your teams and assign tasks to keep projects moving forward, without ever leaving your document.
Review changes with flexibility
You and your stakeholders can reference previous versions of a document at any time and reinstate them.
Accelerate everyday tasks
Get more done without having to leave Docs.
Capture meeting notes with ease and speed
Draft polished, structured meeting notes with event details straight from a
Google Calendar
event using the meeting notes building block in Docs.
Streamline your workflows with electronic signatures
Draft agreements, request signatures, and manage contract templates all in one place with
eSignature
in Docs.
Save time by easily adding content you reuse often
Create a custom building block for pieces of text like a bio, signature, address, and more - simply type ‘@’ to insert instead of endlessly copying and pasting the same content.

Stay safe
Your data is encrypted by default, and an array of
safety measures
– like
advanced client-side encryption
, anti-abuse measures, and privacy controls – keep your
data private
.
Use Docs to improve editing of popular file types
Import popular file types like Microsoft Word documents and PDF files and work with them in Docs. Importing files into Docs unlocks powerful collaborative and assistive features, including comments, action items, and built-in intelligence.
Learn why organizations use Docs
We've completely eliminated concerns over version control and document sharing because we're more certain about who has access to share what and when, all while still being productive.
JK Krug
, Vice President of Digital Employee Experience, Equifax
Learn more
Google Docs is such a convenient tool for international communication. I wrote a press release to share with my team and asked them to make suggestions. The ability to track their edits, accept, reject, or reopen their comments gives me the flexibility I need.
Olha Baburina
, External International Communications Manager, Nova Post
Learn more
Google Workspace with Gemini is becoming a part of our way of life. I personally leveraged Gemini in Google Docs to create a one-page summary of observability and monitoring tools. What Gemini gave me was CTO Ready. The time I get back from Gemini helps me spend more focus hours doing the things that really need my attention.
Amanda Taylor
, Former Vice President of Infrastructure, FinQuery
Learn more
As a non-native English speaker, I’ve used the Help me write prompt in Gemini to deliver more polished written content, such as blogs and emails, faster. I create drafts in Docs and prompt Gemini to improve the grammar and phrasing, then make adjustments to shorten the text and customize the tone to be more formal or casual.
Jessie Xia
, Global Chief Information Officer, Thoughtworks
Learn more
Our client-facing teams use the Help me write feature in Docs to generate ideas and develop outlines. The Shorten feature within Docs, for example, helps with our everyday client-facing messaging. Our clients are busy and we aim for their interactions with us to be the easiest part of their day, which starts with clear and concise communication.
Catelin Carey
, Senior Vice President, WebFX
Read more
Do more with Google Docs
Work, wherever you are
View, present or collaborate in Docs on your mobile phone or tablet, available in the
App Store
and
Play Store
, or connect from your computer.
Stay productive, even offline
Access, create, and edit documents without any internet connection, helping you stay productive anywhere.
Seamlessly connect to your other Google apps
Reply to comments directly from Gmail and embed charts from Sheets. Search the web and Drive for content, all in Docs.

Take a look at our FAQs to learn more.
Expand all
Anyone with a Google Account can create in Docs.
However, some features such as joining a Meet call from your documents are only available on Google Workspace plans. See
plans and pricing for organizations
or
Google Workspace Individual
.
You can convert Microsoft Word documents into Google Docs format, or you can
directly edit
Word documents, without having to make a copy. The original file will remain intact.
Co-editing means that multiple people can work on the same document at the same time, without having to send versions back and forth. Sharing settings allows you to control who can view and edit a spreadsheet, and revision history enables you to revert to earlier versions. Also available in Google
Sheets
and

differentiators: Deep integration with Google Workspace (Gmail, Drive, Chat, Meet, Calendar)
Gemini AI synthesizes context from Drive, Gmail, and Chat to build drafts
Real-time browser-native collaborative editing at scale
Smart canvas / smart chips for structured cross-app referencing
Massive Marketplace ecosystem of add-ons

praised: Best-in-class real-time collaboration
Deep Workspace integration (Gmail, Drive, Chat, Meet)
Reliable browser-based access anywhere
Free tier for personal use
complaints: Bugs like crashes on unusual input ('And. And. And. And. And.' crash)
Concerns about content moderation / inappropriate-word warnings
Email virus/phishing concerns via shared docs
Privacy concerns driving alternatives (e.g. Airborn)

free_tier.limits: Free personal Google account tier; Workspace paid tiers cap at 300 users (Starter/Standard/Plus)

tiers.name: Business Starter
tiers.headline_inclusions: 30 GB pooled storage, custom email, Gemini in Gmail, 100-participant meetings

tiers.name: Business Standard
tiers.headline_inclusions: 2 TB, Gemini across Gmail/Docs/Meet, eSignature, mail merge, 150-participant meetings

tiers.name: Business Plus
tiers.headline_inclusions: 5 TB, eDiscovery, Vault, Secure LDAP, advanced endpoint mgmt, 500-participant meetings

tiers.name: Enterprise
tiers.headline_inclusions: 5 TB+, S/MIME, DLP, context-aware access, data regions, AI Classification, 1000-participant meetings

pricing.notes: Starter/Standard/Plus capped at 300 users; Enterprise unlimited. 20% off first 3 months intro promo; introductory pricing only for first 20 users for 12 months. 16% savings on annual commitment. All prices billed monthly, USD.

landing.title: OneNote
landing.text:
OneNote
OneNote
Word
Excel
PowerPoint
OneNote
See plans & pricing
Sign in
Microsoft OneNote
Your digital notebook
One cross-functional notebook for all your note taking needs.
Sign up for free
Sign in
From notes to polished work, faster with Copilot Notebooks.
Add your content to a notebook to think and create with Copilot based on what matters to you or your team. From insights about your files and emails, to audio overviews, Copilot Notebooks keep you caught up and moving forward.
Sketch, annotate, and highlight freely
OneNote lets you combine the power of digital ink with the natural feel of a pen to help you sketch out your inspirations.
Share and Collaborate
Great minds don't always think alike, but they can share ideas and create together in OneNote.
Never miss a detail with voice transcription
Voice transcription makes it easier than ever to efficiently capture your notes, focus on what's important, and review your content later.
OneNote in Education
Teachers can use OneNote to organize lesson plans in searchable digital notebooks, and staff can create a sharable content library. Encourage students to handwrite notes and sketch diagrams.
Sticky Notes in OneNote
The New Sticky Notes experience is a fresh feature from OneNote to help you remember more seamlessly than ever. With quick capture and automatic recall of the notes when you revisit the same source, remembering what matters just got easier.

Overview
Uplevel your notes with smart features and powerful AI
Copilot in OneNote
As your notetaking partner, Copilot in OneNote uses your prompts to draft plans, generate ideas, create lists, and organize information.
Learn what’s possible with Copilot
Sketch, annotate, and highlight freely
Combine the power of digital ink with the natural feel of a pen to help you sketch out your inspirations.
Share and collaborate
Great minds don’t always think alike, but they can share ideas and create together in OneNote.
Never miss a detail with voice transcription
Voice transcription makes it easier than ever to efficiently capture your notes, focus on what’s important, and review your content later.
OneNote in education
Teachers can use OneNote to organize lesson plans in searchable digital notebooks, and staff can create a shareable content library. Encourage students to handwrite notes and sketch diagrams.

analysis.name: Microsoft OneNote
analysis.value_proposition: One cross-functional notebook for all your note-taking needs, with Copilot-assisted drafting, ink, and voice capture across devices.
analysis.target_segment: consumer / education / SMB / enterprise (bundled via Microsoft 365)
analysis.ideal_customer: Microsoft 365 users — students, teachers, and knowledge workers who want a free-form notebook integrated with Office, OneDrive, and Copilot

features.name: digital notebook
features.description: Cross-functional notebook for all note-taking needs across devices
features.evidence_quote: One cross-functional notebook for all your note taking needs.

features.name: copilot notebooks
features.description: AI-powered notebook that ingests content to draft, ideate, and generate audio overviews
features.evidence_quote: From notes to polished work, faster with Copilot Notebooks.

features.name: copilot in onenote
features.description: AI assistant that drafts plans, generates ideas, creates lists, and organizes notes from prompts
features.evidence_quote: Copilot in OneNote uses your prompts to draft plans, generate ideas, create lists, and organize information.

features.name: audio overviews
features.description: Copilot-generated audio summaries of notebook content
features.evidence_quote: to audio overviews, Copilot Notebooks keep you caught up

features.name: summarize notes
features.description: Copilot can summarize existing OneNote content

features.name: rewrite with copilot
features.description: AI-powered rewriting of note text

features.name: to-do lists and tasks
features.description: Create to-do lists and tasks inside notebooks
features.evidence_quote: Create a to-do list and tasks

features.name: project plans
features.description: Build project plans directly in OneNote

features.name: digital ink
features.description: Sketch, annotate, and highlight with pen-like digital ink
features.evidence_quote: OneNote lets you combine the power of digital ink with the natural feel of a pen

features.name: handwriting
features.description: Take handwritten notes on touch/pen devices

features.name: annotations and highlighting
features.description: Freeform annotation and highlighting on pages

features.name: voice transcription
features.description: Transcribe voice recordings into notes automatically
features.evidence_quote: Never miss a detail with voice transcription

features.name: sharing and collaboration
features.description: Share notebooks and collaborate with others in real time
features.evidence_quote: they can share ideas and create together in OneNote

features.name: notebook sync
features.description: Sync notebooks across devices via OneDrive

features.name: cross-device access
features.description: Access notes on PC, Mac, web, iOS, Android

features.name: mobile app
features.description: Native OneNote mobile apps for iOS and Android
features.evidence_quote: Get the OneNote mobile app — Download today for iOS or Android

features.name: sticky notes
features.description: Quick-capture sticky notes with automatic contextual recall
features.evidence_quote: New Sticky Notes experience... quick capture and automatic recall of the notes

features.name: onenote clipper
features.description: Web clipper to save web content into a notebook

features.name: notebooks organization
features.description: Notebook / section / page hierarchy to stay organized

features.name: search
features.description: Search across notebooks including within notes
features.evidence_quote: organize lesson plans in searchable digital notebooks

features.name: ocr from images
features.description: Copy text from pictures and file printouts
features.evidence_quote: Copy text from pictures and file printouts

features.name: math equations
features.description: Convert and solve math equations inside notes

features.name: password protection
features.description: Protect note sections with a password

features.name: keyboard shortcuts
features.description: Extensive keyboard shortcut support

features.name: class notebooks
features.description: Education-focused notebooks for lesson plans and shared content
features.evidence_quote: Teachers can use OneNote to organize lesson plans in searchable digital notebooks

features.name: staff content library
features.description: Shareable content library for education staff
features.evidence_quote: staff can create a sharable content library

features.name: onedrive cloud storage
features.description: Notes stored in OneDrive; 1TB with Microsoft 365 Personal
features.evidence_quote: 1 TB (1000 GB) of secure cloud storage

features.name: office suite bundling
features.description: Bundled with Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook desktop apps
features.evidence_quote: Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and OneNote desktop apps with Microsoft Copilot

features.name: microsoft teams integration
features.description: Included Teams for chat/calls/collaboration with Copilot
features.evidence_quote: Microsoft Teams with Copilot... to call, chat, and collaborate

features.name: ransomware protection
features.description: OneDrive ransomware protection for files and photos
features.evidence_quote: OneDrive ransomware protection for your photos and files

features.name: microsoft defender
features.description: Advanced security for identity, personal data, and devices
features.evidence_quote: Microsoft Defender advanced security for your identity, personal data, and devices

features.name: identity theft monitoring
features.description: Identity theft monitoring (US only) via Defender
features.evidence_quote: Identity theft monitoring is only available in the United States

features.name: ai image creation
features.description: AI image generation in Copilot with higher usage than free
features.evidence_quote: Higher usage for AI image creation in Copilot

features.name: work file copilot access
features.description: Use personal Copilot with work files under enterprise multi-account access
features.evidence_quote: Use Copilot in select apps with work files in a secure way

features.name: office add-ins marketplace
features.description: Third-party add-ins available via Microsoft AppSource

features.name: multi-device install
features.description: Use on up to five devices simultaneously per subscription
features.evidence_quote: Use on up to five devices simultaneously

features.name: web app
features.description: OneNote available as a web app in addition to desktop/mobile

differentiators: Free-form canvas combining typing, ink, and voice on any device
Deep Microsoft 365 / Office integration and OneDrive sync
Copilot Notebooks with audio overviews grounded on user content
Strong education footprint (class notebooks, staff content library)
Bundled at no marginal cost inside Microsoft 365 subscriptions

recent_ships.what: Copilot Notebooks — grounded drafting and audio overviews from user content
recent_ships.source: From notes to polished work, faster with Copilot Notebooks.

recent_ships.what: New Sticky Notes experience with quick capture and automatic recall
recent_ships.source: The New Sticky Notes experience is a fresh feature from OneNote

recent_ships.what: Voice transcription for notes
recent_ships.source: Never miss a detail with voice transcription

recent_ships.what: End of support for OneNote 2016 and OneNote 2019
recent_ships.source: End of support for OneNote 2016 and OneNote 2019

praised: Broad tips/potential for power users ('Tips to Unlock the Potential of Microsoft OneNote' — 92 upvotes on HN)
Now free and available on Mac, lowering the barrier vs. Evernote
complaints: Missing basic editing: 'Microsoft OneNote does not support replace text feature'
Security abuse vector: 'Microsoft OneNote is still being used to flood devices with malware'
Users seek open alternatives: 'OpenNote – an open web-based alternative to Microsoft OneNote and EverNote' (91 upvotes)
Reports of note corruption: 'Microsoft OneNote Problem/Issue screwing my notes!'

free_tier.limits: OneNote free with Microsoft account; ~5GB free OneDrive storage; limited Copilot usage

tiers.name: Microsoft 365 Personal
tiers.headline_inclusions: 1 person, 5 devices, 1TB OneDrive, Word/Excel/PowerPoint/Outlook/OneNote + Copilot + Teams + Defender

pricing.notes: OneNote itself is free with a Microsoft account; paid tiers are Microsoft 365 subscriptions (Personal, Family, Business, Enterprise). Only Personal tier fully visible in captured pricing page snippet. AI features tied to subscription owner with usage limits; work-file Copilot access requires employer enterprise subscription with multi-account access enabled (excludes government tenants). Yearly billing option offered but discount % not shown in captured snippet.

Frequently Asked Questions
How long is the free trial for paid plans?
Unless otherwise specified, free trials of Evernote last for 7 days. If you cancel before the trial ends, your credit card will not be charged
How do I switch plans?
To upgrade your plan from Free to Starter or Advanced:
Go to your
account settings in Evernote Web
, then click ‘Manage Subscription’ on the ‘Account Summary’ page.
Select the plan you wish to upgrade to (Starter or Advanced).
Enter your credit card or PayPal information and choose a billing frequency (monthly or yearly).
To upgrade your plan from Starter to Advanced:
Go to your
account settings in Evernote Web
, then click ‘Evernote Starter’ at the top of the ‘Account Summary’ page.
Select the Advanced plan.
Enter your credit card or PayPal information and choose a billing frequency (monthly or yearly).
The process for downgrading your plan to Free depends on your payment method. For detailed instructions, please see
this Help & Learning article
.
Note: If you are subscribed to Evernote through iTunes or Google Play, you will need to manage your subscription through them.
How do I change my payment method?
To change your payment method:
Go to your
account settings in Evernote Web
, then click ‘Billing’ on the left side of the page.
Click ‘Update’ next to your existing payment method.
Enter your new credit card or PayPal information.
Click 'Update.'
Note: If you subscribed to Evernote through iTunes or Google Play, you will need to manage your subscription through them.
Do you offer a student discount?
Yes! Eligible college students can get 40% off a one-year subscription to Evernote Starter.
Learn more
.
Is there a free trial of Evernote Enterprise?
You can try Enterprise free for 14 days. Visit our
Enterprise trial page
to get started.
I have an Evernote account. How do I upgrade to the Enterprise plan?
To upgrade to Enterprise, please
contact sales
. They’ll be glad to get you up and running and answer any further questions you might have.
How do I change my payment method?
To change your payment method:
Go to your account settings in Evernote Web, then click ‘Billing’ on the left side of the page
Click ‘Update’ next to your existing payment method
Enter your new credit card information
Click ‘Update.’
I'm a student. Should I get Evernote Enterprise for my work group?
While you can use Evernote Enterprise with your group, our Advanced plan also features easy ways to share information with others. Visit our
Student Discount page
to see if Evernote Advanced is right for you.
