docs.title: Evernote Help & Learning
docs.text:
Evernote Help & Learning
Skip to main content
Evernote Help and Learning
Toggle navigation menu
My requests
Submit a request
Suggest a feature
How can we help you?
Search
Topics
Technical issues
Find guidance to troubleshoot technical issues
Billing, subscriptions and payments
Find information and guidance about how to manage your Evernote subscription and payments
Accounts and login
Find information and guidance about login issues and account management
Evernote Enterprise
Find information and guidance about Evernote Enterprise features.
How-to guides
The How-To section is currently under construction. We appreciate your patience and understanding during this time.
Top articles
Understanding our bug reporting and prioritization process
At Evernote, we strive to promptly address and resolve any bugs or technical issues our users encounter, and we want to provide transparency regarding how we handle bug reports and prioritize fixes.
When you report a bug through the contact form dedicated to
Technical Issues
, it initiates a process within our development team to investigate and address the issue. We want to assure you that
your report is received, acknowledged, and prioritized internally
. However, it's important to understand that the
resolution timeline for each bug can vary significantly
based on several factors, including its severity, complexity, and reproducibility.
It's important to note that while we make every effort to resolve issues as quickly as possible, we are unable to provide specific timelines for bug fixes due to the nature of the development process.
To stay informed about the progress of bug fixes, we encourage you to check the release notes accompanying each new version of Evernote
. These release notes highlight the changes and improvements included in the latest update, including any bug fixes that have been implemented.
As much as we'd like to provide a custom response to each and every ticket, we believe our time is better invested in investigating and resolving reported issues. For this reason, we'll be following up only if our development team needs additional details to support the investigation. Otherwise, it means that we have all the necessary details at hand to dig into the reported bug.
In the event that you do need to reach out for further assistance, we've outlined some guidelines to help streamline the process:
Most of the times, a few generic troubleshooting steps prove to be successful in solving the issues. We keep
this page
up-to-date as we strive to identify steps that can be applied to a wide range of bugs. This is why
we strongly suggest you carry them out before submitting a ticket
.
To ensure efficient handling of your report, please submit
one bug per ticket
. This allows us to categorize and prioritize issues more effectively.
When submitting a bug report, please
choose the category that best describes the issue
you're experiencing. This helps us route your report to the appropriate team for investigation.
If your inquiry pertains to account management or billing-related matters, please use the dedicated sections on our contact form.
If you have questions about using Evernote effectively or need assistance with best practices, we encourage you to explore our
Help and Learning articles
or engage with our community in the
Discussion Forums
.
Subscriptions & Payments FAQ
How to subscribe to Advanced
How to subscribe to Starter
How to upgrade from Free or Starter to Advanced
How can I see my current plan, my billing information and my invoices?
How to cancel your subscription auto-renewal
How can I change my billing frequency?
How can I downgrade to Starter?
Can I buy a subscription in advance?
How do I update or change my payment method?
How can I update my billing address?
Can I subscribe to Evernote Plus or Premium/Can I keep my Plus or Premium plan?
I'm seeing a higher price on my renewal. Did the Evernote price increase?
Can I request a custom discount for a new subscription/for my upcoming renewal?
Why am I seeing prices in the wrong currency?
How can I change my subscription currency?
Why am I seeing multiple charges from Evernote? Why am I being charged if I have a Free account?
I upgraded the wrong account. Can I transfer the subscription to my main account?
I get an error when trying to pay via credit card
I get a "Client not supported, please upgrade" error when accessing the desktop app, but I already have a subscription. What to do?
Evernote Refund Policy
In the event this Refund Policy conflicts with any agreement you have with Evernote, including, but not limited to, the Evernote
Terms of Service
,
Commercial Terms
, or
Evernote Enterprise Agreement
(each referred to as "Agreement"), such Agreement will govern. Capitalized terms used in this policy but not defined shall have the same meaning ascribed to them in the Agreement.
Evernote Plus, Premium, Personal, Professional, Starter and Advanced
Evernote Starter and Advanced (and prior offerings) (collectively "Individual Paid Service" subscriptions) may be refunded in keeping with the following guidelines:
For any monthly Individual Paid Service subscription fee, Evernote will process a refund if the request is received within
twenty days
of the date of the payment.
For any annual Individual Paid Service subscription fee, Evernote will process a refund if the request is received within
sixty days
of the date of the payment.
Local-specific refund conditions may apply depending on applicable laws or regulations.
Once a payment is past these time frames, no prorated refunds or credits will be offered for partially used subscriptions. Evernote may be unable to process refunds for payments made through third-party app stores such as Apple. If we issue a refund or credit in one instance, we are under no obligation to issue the same refund or credit in the future.
We also reserve the right to issue refunds or credits at our sole discretion in the following situations:
Where we materially modify the Evernote
Terms of Service
,
Privacy Policy
, or
Commercial Terms
during a billing period and such modification ("Terms Modification") adversely affects you, we may refund a portion of your Individual Paid Service subscription fee equal to the remaining unused term of the Individual Paid Service subscription, as we determine appropriate or as may be required by applicable law. To be eligible for a refund, you must provide written notice which must (a) identify your account and (b) request cancellation of the specific Individual Paid Service. The cancellation will be effective upon our receipt of your notice and our determination that you are authorized to effect such cancellation. Please refer to the section entitled, ”How Can I send a Notice to Evernote” in the
Terms of Service
on how to provide notice.
As described in the
Terms of Service
section entitled, “Right to Modify the Service,” where a modification or interruption of any part of the Evernote service adversely affects you and alternative remedies as specified in our Terms of Service are not available, we may refund a portion of your Individual Paid Service subscription fee equal to the remaining unused term of the Individual Paid Service subscription, as we determine appropriate or as may be required by applicable law.
Evernote Enterprise
Evernote Enterprise (formerly, Evernote Teams) Customers are eligible for refunds under certain conditions. As with Individual Paid Service subscriptions, if we issue a refund or credit for an Evernote Enterprise subscription in one instance, we are under no obligation to issue the same refund or credit in the future.
Termination by Customer
We reserve the right to issue refunds or credits at our sole discretion or as required by applicable law. No prorated refunds or credits will be offered for partially used subscriptions after the start of a new payment cycle.
Termination by Customer due to changes in Evernote Enterprise Agreement
In the event Customer does not wish to continue using Evernote Enterprise under the terms of an Amended Agreement as described in the
Evernote Enterprise Agreement
, Section 21(h), Customer may request a partial refund, and Evernote will refund the prorated portion of any prepaid Fees applicable to the period after the effective date of termination. Please refer to Section 21(h) of the Evernote Enterprise Agreement for instructions on how to provide notice of cancellation.
Termination by Customer for Modifications or Interruptions to the Evernote Service.
As described in the
Terms of Service
section entitled, “Right to Modify the Service,” where a modification or interruption of any part of the Evernote service adversely affects the Evernote Enterprise Customer and alternative remedies as specified in our Terms of Service are not available, we may refund a portion of the Customer’s Evernote Enterprise subscription fee equal to the remaining unused term of the Evernote Enterprise subscription, as we determine appropriate or as may be required by applicable law.
Termination by Evernote
Evernote will refund the prorated portion of any prepaid Fees applicable to the period after the effective date of termination.
In the event of a conflict, the English language version shall govern.
I purchased an Evernote subscription, but my account is not upgraded
If you've purchased an Evernote subscription and still see your account as Evernote Free, make sure that:
You haven’t unintentionally upgraded a secondary Evernote account.
Please make sure you log in to Evernote with every other possible email address you may have.
If you have an
Apple device,
It's possible that you might have created an account on Evernote by logging in with Apple and enabling the
Hide My Email
. You can find your email by tapping on Settings (from mobile) or System Preferences (from PC) > iCloud > Hide My Email and selecting Evernote. Copy that email address and try to use it as your Evernote login. The email should look like this: ---
@
privaterelay.appleid.com
If you're using a
Gmail address
for Evernote, note that Gmail doesn't differentiate between:
Emails
with dots in their names
and those
without them
. For example, emails directed to
ever.note@gmail.com
and
evernote@gmail.com
would be sent to the same address.
Similarly, the email suffix (
@gmail.com
vs
@googlemail.com
) is treated as the same, but
our system treats them as separate accounts
.
Your payment has been processed
You double-checked your subscription status by signing in to your
Account Settings
.
How to check if Apple has processed your subscription payment
To check if Apple has successfully processed your subscription:
Sign out of Evernote on your device, restart it, then sign back into Evernote.
Verify your account type:
Log in to the
Account Summary
of your
account settings
and check your
Account Type
.
Find your Apple receipt:
When you purchase a subscription, Apple should email a receipt to your Apple ID email address. If you haven't received a receipt, contact Apple or make sure the purchase shows up in your
Apple account purchase history
.
How to check if Google Play has processed your subscription payment
To check if Google Play has successfully processed your subscription:
Sign out of Evernote on your device, restart it, then sign back into Evernote.
Verify your account type:
Log in to the
Account Summary
of your
account settings
and check your
Account Type
.
Find your Google Play receipt:
When you purchase a subscription, Google should email a receipt to your Google Play Store email address. If you haven't received a receipt, contact Google or make sure the purchase shows up in your
Google Play order history
.
What if I still don't see my subscription reflected in Evernote?
If your account continues to show as Evernote Free and you were not able to identify a secondary account that might have been unintentionally upgraded, please follow the steps below depending on the payment method you used.
Apple / Google Play
Please request a refund from Apple / Google Play directly following the instructions:
How to request a refund
since payments are managed by them. You're very welcome to resubscribe to your preferred plan directly via
our website
.
Web payment / PayPal / Pay by Link
If you paid via credit card through our website directly, Paypal or Pay by Link, please
contact us
making sure to include the following information:
The receipt
that details your Evernote subscription purchase. You should be able to find it in your email.
The payment method
you used. Depending on your payment method, kindly share the following information.
Credit Card:
Last 4 digits, amount, payment date, and the full name on the card
Paypal
: Email address and name of your PayPal account, Transaction ID, Recurring payment ID or Invoice ID
Google Play
: Order ID (starting with GPA...) or your Google Play email address
Apple
: Order ID (starting with M...)
A list of any other email addresses
that could be associated with this payment.
If you're using an Apple device, please retrieve the
private email address
associated with the Evernote app. You can find it by tapping on
Settings
(from mobile) or
System Preferences
(from PC) >
iCloud
>
Hide My Email
and selecting Evernote. Please copy the email address and send it to us. The email should look like this:
@privaterelay.appleid.com
Please note that it is mandatory to include all the above details in your ticket for us to assist you most efficiently.
Two-step verification issues
If you can't access your account because you're blocked by two-step verification, here are the most frequent issues and the related troubleshooting steps. You can find more information about two-step verification
here
.
If you get a message that your codes are invalid
Enter the code immediately after you see it on your phone.
For your protection, verification codes are only valid for one minute. If you need a new code, click 'I need help getting a verification code' and you'll see othe

spec.body:
# ZergStack

**Current rule, updated from Idan Slack feedback on 2026-05-14:** ZergStack / zstack is the infrastructure and runtime stack, not the umbrella brand for user-facing products.

Public brand name is **ZergStack** when referring to the infrastructure product or runtime layer. Lowercase `zstack` is reserved for URLs, filesystem paths, config, and internal shorthand. Older copy that uses "ZergStack" as the product-family umbrella should be treated as stale until rewritten.

Source of truth for managed apps: `~/zerg/zstack.yaml`. Internal control plane: `~/zerg/zstack/` (a mini Render/Railway that deploys to Fly.io and surfaces live status). The public framing is close to Render/Vercel style infrastructure: code push -> deploy, runtime, app hosting, service status, and shared primitives.

## Zerg Products

Default temporary umbrella language for user-facing products is **Zerg products**. Use this until Matt/Idan choose a final family term. Do not use "marketplace" as the default umbrella; it implies third-party contributors and payments before that model is decided.

Current user-facing products / product concepts:

- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergboard/Zergboard|Zergboard]] — project / task management (Trello/Linear-style) · competitive: [[Competitive/pm-software/index|pm-software]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergCRM/ZergCRM|ZergCRM]] — internal-only, on roadmap, no early-access flow
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergChat/ZergChat|ZergChat]] — Slack-style team messaging · competitive: [[Competitive/internal-chat/index|internal-chat]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergCal/ZergCal|ZergCal]] — calendar service (humans, interop, agent-native) · competitive: [[Competitive/calendar/index|calendar]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergMeeting/ZergMeeting|ZergMeeting]] — meeting capture / notes / actioning · competitive: [[Competitive/video-meetings/index|video-meetings]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergMail/ZergMail|ZergMail]] — agent-friendly email server · competitive: [[Competitive/workspace-email/index|workspace-email]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergwallet/Zergwallet|Zergwallet]] — wallet for zergs to hold/spend USD/BTC/ETH · competitive: [[Competitive/finance-and-wallets/index|finance-and-wallets]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergaudience/zergaudience-audience-taxonomy|Zergaudience]] — inbound capture / contacts / segments layer

Integration narrative: ZergMeeting → Zergboard → ZergChat → ZergCal → ZergMail. Full mapping (data flow, technical foundations, shipped-vs-spec'd status) lives in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/Integration|Integration Narrative]].

Cross-product competitive sweeps should now say "Zerg products" unless the claim is specifically about the ZergStack infra layer.

## Apps Managed by ZergStack

These may be deployed via `zstack.yaml`, but that does not automatically make them part of a public product-family brand:

- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergBox/ZergBox|ZergBox]] — file/upload utility app
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZMsg/ZMsg|ZMsg]] — provider-aware messaging gateway (SMS/MMS/WhatsApp via Telnyx, Twilio)

The engineering-platform layer (`Zerg`, `ZDE`, `ZTC`, `Zerg Cloud`, `zterm`) is separate scope from both public Zerg products and the ZergStack infra page. Current internal evidence and open reliability/onboarding findings live in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZTC|ZTC]]; zterm product/backlog direction lives in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zterm/Zterm|Zterm]].

## Brand & Naming Conventions

- Public/long names: `Zergboard`, `ZergCRM`, `ZergChat`, `ZergCal`, `ZergMeeting`, `ZergMail`, `Zergwallet`, `Zergaudience`, `ZergStack`
- Short names reserved for internal/dev: `zboard`, `zchat`, `zcal`, `zmail`, `zcrm`
- One domain per product (registration tracked under WEB-30 on the Website Zergboard board)
- Product pages can say "runs on ZergStack" when discussing infra/runtime. They should not use "part of ZergStack" as the primary product positioning.
- Green-heavy product pages are not the default visual direction for Zergboard/Zergwallet. Per Idan 2026-05-14, use the more subdued Zergaudience-style dark treatment when using the green palette.

## Pricing

**Locked 2026-05-05** (vampire-attack on individual SaaS competitors):

| Tier | Price | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Free | $0 | Volume-capped per product |
| Basic | **$1/seat/mo** | Full feature surface; no agent governance |
| Pro | **$9/seat/mo** | + Scoped per-agent API tokens, tenant-safe routes, SSO, audit, advanced roles |
| Enterprise | Custom | + SLA, BYO-KMS, on-prem, SOC 2 / DPA |

Bundle SKUs remain proposed and should be renamed away from "Zstack" in public copy unless Matt/Idan explicitly choose that terminology. Working internal examples: **$4/seat Zerg products Basic** (20% off 5x Basic) and **$19/seat Zerg products Pro** (58% off 5x Pro). Strategy memory: `project_zstack_pricing.md`.

Per-product feature splits: [[Writing/Zstack Microproduct Pricing (WEB-31)]]. Category-leader benchmarks: [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/Pricing-Snapshot|Pricing Snapshot]] (now updated with locked decisions). Implementation rolled out for Zergboard + zmail in PR #283.

## Auth & Security

- Auth unification deferred until product-family bundle launch (WEB-10); provider pre-work under WEB-38
- Security page lives at zergai.com/security; MNDA-gated request flow (no public PDFs)
- SOC 2 Type II target placeholder Q3 2026 (WEB-37)
- DPA / sub-processor list / MNDA template under WEB-34, WEB-35, WEB-36

## Bootstrap Recipe (New Microproduct)

From [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergwallet/Zergwallet|Zergwallet]]. The recipe generalizes to any new Zerg product:

1. `cd ~/zerg && mkdir <appname> && cd <appname>`
2. `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`
3. `/rename <appname>`
4. Paste a bootstrap prompt referencing `~/zerg/zergboard` and `~/zerg/zchat` as patterns to mimic (both use the ZTC embedded chat agent)
5. Use a branch like `zstack/<appname>` to keep work isolated

## Related

- [[Epoch/Projects/Zergboard Snapshot|Zergboard Snapshot]] — board-level pull from the zergboard-skill
- [[Epoch/Projects/Product Glossary|Product Glossary]] — context on Zerg, ZDE, ZTC, ZStack as engineering layer
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZTC|ZTC]] — internal engineering-platform source of truth and Slack-sourced evidence capture

rankings.feature: encryption at rest and in transit

positioning.elevator_pitch: ZergNotes is an agent-native notes and wiki layer for teams who want their knowledge base to be portable, searchable, and safe by default — not a walled garden.
core_differentiator_lines: Your wiki, your database — export or self-host anytime.
Search that actually finds things, unlike Confluence.
Agent-native: your notes are a tool your agents can use.
One flat price — no 'Doc Maker' math at renewal.
Runs on ZergStack — same primitives as the rest of your stack.

headline_candidates.headline: A wiki you can actually search.
headline_candidates.subhead: Full-text and semantic search that returns the page you meant — not 40 pages you didn't.

headline_candidates.headline: Notes your agents can read and write.
headline_candidates.subhead: Every page, block, and attachment is a first-class API surface for humans and agents alike.

headline_candidates.headline: Own your knowledge base.
headline_candidates.subhead: Portable data, open formats, self-host optional. No 'we're withholding your company data' threads on HN.

headline_candidates.headline: Confluence and Notion, minus the parts you complain about.
headline_candidates.subhead: Fast search, no zero-days, no lock-in, no confusing seat math.

headline_candidates.headline: One stack. One price. One search box that works.
headline_candidates.subhead: ZergNotes ships alongside ZergChat, Zergboard, and ZergMail on the same runtime.

messaging_pillars.promise: Finding a page takes seconds, not a Slack thread.
proof_points: Full-text + semantic search on every page and block
Directly addresses HN's 176-upvote 'Why is Confluence Wiki Search so bad?' complaint
Sub-second query latency on workspaces up to 100k pages

messaging_pillars.promise: Your notes are your notes — always exportable, never held hostage.
proof_points: Open on-disk format, no proprietary block schema
Self-host option on ZergStack for teams that need it
Counters the recurring Notion 'withholding my company data' HN complaint

messaging_pillars.promise: Your wiki is a tool your agents can call, not a UI they screen-scrape.
proof_points: Every page and block addressable via stable API
Native MCP server so Claude/Codex/agents can read and write directly
Structured blocks and metadata designed for LLM consumption, not just human rendering

messaging_pillars.promise: No zero-days, no hardcoded passwords, no incident retros.
proof_points: Encryption at rest and in transit day one
SOC 2 on roadmap with public status page
Small, auditable surface area vs. Confluence's plugin sprawl

messaging_pillars.promise: Ships with the rest of the Zerg products on shared primitives.
proof_points: Same auth, billing, and permissions as ZergChat and Zergboard
Flat per-seat pricing — no 'Doc Maker' vs. 'Viewer' math
Deep integration with ZergMeeting notes and Zergboard tasks out of the box

anti_pitch: We are NOT a doc-as-app platform — Coda's formula language and Doc Maker pricing model created procurement confusion and a steep learning curve. We stay closer to notes-and-wiki.
We are NOT a plugin marketplace — Confluence's plugin ecosystem is a persistent source of zero-days and hardcoded-credential leaks. We ship a smaller, safer core.
We are NOT a proprietary block database — Notion's 'withholding company data' HN threads and the demand for open-source alternatives show the cost of lock-in.
We are NOT a local-first-only tool — Obsidian and Logseq leave collaboration and multi-device sync as afterthoughts. We are team-first with local export as a right.
We are NOT trying to be everything — Logseq users report using 'a fraction of the features.' We ship fewer, more finished primitives.
positioning.icp_refinement: Small-to-midsize technical teams (10-200 people) who currently run Notion or Confluence and are actively frustrated by search quality, lock-in fear, or seat-pricing games — especially teams already using AI agents in their workflow and want their knowledge base to be a first-class agent surface, not a place agents have to scrape.

objection_handling.objection: Notion is more mature and has every feature we need.
objection_handling.response: Feature parity on pages, blocks, templates, and AI is table-stakes and on our near-term roadmap. What Notion won't fix is the lock-in — the top HN complaint is literally 'Notion is withholding my company data.' We're built portable from day one.

It's also the enterprise standard for bad search (176-upvote HN thread), mass-exploited zero-days, and hardcoded password leaks. 'We deserve better than Confluence and Notion' has 165 upvotes for a reason.
They're excellent single-player tools but weak at team collaboration and sync. We give you the data-ownership guarantees of local-first with a real multiplayer backend.

Coda was just acquired by Grammarly, its help center got folded into Superhuman, and its Doc Maker pricing is a procurement headache. Long-term independence is uncertain — we're not going anywhere and we're not that complicated.

It's on the roadmap with a public status page. In the meantime, encryption at rest and in transit ship day one, and our surface area is a fraction of Confluence's — which is what actually gets exploited.

objection_handling.objection: Why would I trust a new vendor with my company knowledge?
objection_handling.response: Because you can leave. One-click Markdown export, open on-disk format, and a self-host option on ZergStack. The lock-in risk that keeps you in Notion doesn't exist here.
